# MCP Runtime Documentation
> Open-source, Kubernetes-native platform for deploying, governing, and brokering MCP servers. Policy enforcement per tool call, multi-team isolation, and a full audit trail — no YAML required.

## Getting started

- Quickstart: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/quickstart/ — Deploy a governed server in 10 minutes using the live platform. No cluster needed. Includes binary install, server init with tool discovery, grant, adapter proxy.
- Getting Started (self-host): https://docs.mcpruntime.org/getting-started/ — Install MCP Runtime on your own Kubernetes cluster. Binary install, cluster readiness, setup, health check.
- Concepts: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/concepts/ — Core abstractions: MCPServer, MCPAccessGrant, MCPAgentSession, gateway, adapter, trust levels, side effects, platform mode, scopes, .mcp/servers.yaml.

## Developer guide

- Publish an MCP Server: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/publish-mcp-server/ — Write metadata, build and push a Docker image, deploy, verify reconciliation, observe traffic.
- Agent Adapters: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/agent-adapters/ — HTTP proxy and stdio shim for agents. Session injection, auto-refresh, identity headers, environment variables reference.
- Multi-Team Isolation: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/multi-team/ — Namespace-per-team, RBAC, NetworkPolicy, team fields on MCPServer/Grant/Session, cross-team access patterns.

## Learning path (structured modules)

- Learn overview: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/learn/ — Three-module structured path: Concepts → First server → Multi-team.
- Module 1 — Core concepts: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/learn/module-1-concepts/ — MCPServer, Grant, Session, Gateway, Adapter explained with decision tables and check-your-understanding.
- Module 2 — First governed server: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/learn/module-2-first-server/ — End-to-end hands-on: init, validate, build, push, deploy, grant, adapter, analytics. Includes break-it exercises.
- Module 3 — Multi-team setup: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/learn/module-3-multi-team/ — Two teams, cross-team grants, session management, production checklist.

## Operator guide

- Deployment Targets: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/deployment-targets/ — Choose the right install shape for k3s, EKS, GKE, AKS, and other distributions.
- Cluster Readiness: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/cluster-readiness/ — Distribution-specific prerequisites: registry trust, DNS, TLS, storage, ingress, MetalLB.
- k3s On-Prem Cluster: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/k3s-on-prem-cluster/ — Four-node k3s cluster topology, networking, and node configuration for a production-style setup.
- k3s Deployment Runbook: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/k3s-deployment-runbook/ — Environment variables, deploy/redeploy/cleanup scripts, rollout operations for k3s.
- Runtime: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/runtime/ — Operator reconciliation, CRD lifecycle, Deployment/Service/Ingress outputs, traffic model, gateway sidecar, rollout settings.
- Sentinel: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/sentinel/ — Gateway policy enforcement, grant/session evaluation, analytics ingest pipeline, ClickHouse, observability stack, CLI operations.
- Troubleshooting: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/troubleshooting/ — Common errors and fixes: tool_side_effect_unknown, 401 registry, ImagePullBackOff, session expired, Kafka topic missing, stale Certificate, namespace Terminating.

## Reference

- CLI reference: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/cli/ — Every command: auth, server, registry, access, adapter, team, sentinel, setup, cluster, bootstrap. Flags, defaults, examples.
- API and CRDs: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/api/ — MCPServer, MCPAccessGrant, MCPAgentSession field reference, gateway headers, HTTP endpoints.
- Architecture: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/architecture/ — System design: control plane, registry, broker, operator, Sentinel services, request flow.

## Contributor guide

- Contributor overview: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/contributor/ — How to contribute, repo structure, PR process.
- Local Kind and Test Mode: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/contributor/local-kind/ — Kind cluster setup, test-mode install, local registry, port-forward.
- Service Iteration: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/contributor/service-iteration/ — Iterating on API/UI/gateway services without full rebuild.
- Runtime MCP Testing: https://docs.mcpruntime.org/contributor/runtime-mcp-testing/ — Smoke tests and governed-access verification on a local cluster.
- Troubleshooting (contributor): https://docs.mcpruntime.org/contributor/troubleshooting/ — Development-environment specific issues.

## Key concepts for AI assistants

- An MCPServer CRD describes a running MCP server. The operator creates Deployment, Service, and Ingress automatically.
- An MCPAccessGrant defines which agent can call which tools on which server, at what trust level, with which side effects.
- An MCPAgentSession is a time-bounded, revocable token carrying the human's consented trust level.
- The gateway sidecar enforces grants + sessions on every tool call. No grant = denied. Expired/revoked session = denied.
- The adapter proxy injects governance headers and creates/refreshes sessions automatically.
- Tool names in .mcp/servers.yaml must exactly match the server's registered tool names. Mismatch causes tool_side_effect_unknown.
- Trust levels: low (read-only) / medium (writes) / high (destructive).
- Side effects: read / write / destructive.
- Platform modes: tenant (per-team) / org (org-wide) / public (anonymous).
- Scopes for deploy/push: tenant / org / public.
